def: s.m. = S کنکتي कंकती kaṅkatī, s.f. A comb, hair-comb.
adj. Crop-ear; earless. kan-kaṭā , n. m. One who cuts off another's ears. Dekh! nahīṅ māntā, bulāūṅ kan-kaṭe ko? Hin. Wom. (Said to frighten a child). kan-kaṭī , n. f. A disorder in the ears.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Ear-cropt, earless; — a person whose ears have been cut off; — (for kān-kāṭā) one who cuts off the ears (of another; syn. kān-kāṭne-wālā).
s.f. A disorder in the ears so called.
more: s. f. A disorder in the ears.

def: s.f. = S کنکت कंकत kaṅkat, s.m. A comb, hair-comb.
s. (dakh.) A sort of paper kite, in raising which they use silk line ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
s.m. A square paper kite (which is flown with silk-line, called nak); — a kind of wild herb, Ipomæa muricata
more: n. m. Dim. kankaiyā , n. f. 1. A wild herb; ipomœa muricata , Wat. 2. A square paper kite. kankavvā uṛānā , v. a. To fly a paper kite. kankavvā kāṭnā , v. a. To cut a rival's paper kite. kankavvā laṛānā , v. a. To set one paper kite against another. kankavve-bāz , n. m. One given up to kite-flying.
subs: To set one kite against another, to endeavour to cut the silk-line of a (rival) kite (by entangling the line of one's own kite with it)
s.m. One who is addicted to kite-flying.

def: s.f. Armpit; side, flank (syn. bag̠al).

The armpit ( bagal , 1). 1. Tere sāṛe ne kākh meṅ dabā-ke phek dūṅgo! Rus. You, sister's pimp! I'll crush you under my armpit and throw you off! 2. Navvā dekh le kāhkhe bār! Bhoj. Prov. A barber seen—hair in the armpit. (The sight of the barber is suggestive of hair to be shaven). 3. Jījī! sauk, Rām! kisī ne na de! kākh kā ghūnsā ho saë . Rus. Sister! May Rām ne'er grant a co-wife to any creature! it is like a blow in the armpit.
s.m. Violent effort, straining (as in lifting a heavy load, or at stool, &c.); grunting, grunt; groan, moan; snort; snore.
s.m. A centipede, Scolopendra.
S. n. m. कर्णजलूका Pālī, kannaj alūkā , A centipede.
v.n. To strain; to grunt; to groan, moan; to snort; to snore.
more: v. n. 1. To groan; moan. 2. To strain (at stool, etc.).

def: s. (dakh.) Ear-picker ( Harris ).
s.m. (dialec.) = kākṛā, q.v. s.m. Stiff or firm leather; a strap of leather; thong (of a whip); — a large or stiff wick.
more: s. (dakh.) Wick of a torch ( Harris ).

def: adj. = next, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Abounding in kaṅkar; stony; gravelly; gritty; — stony or gravelly soil.
s. (dakh.) Cucumber ( Harris ): see ککڙي kakṛī.
s.m. A kind of gourd, Momordica miæta.
more: s. m. A kind of gourd. (Momordica mixta: Roxb. ).

def: s.f. Desire, inclination, wish, longing.
adj. & s.m. (f. -iṉī), Desiring, expecting, longing for; — a desirer, an expectant.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Wished, desired, expected, longed for; desirous, longing.
s.f. Wish, desire.
s.m. = kambu-grīv, q.v.
s. m. (dakh.) See کنگره kangura.

s.f. & m. See s.v. kaṇ or kan (and cf. kūt). s.m. The ear; — hearing; heed, regard; — the handle (of a vessel); helm or rudder (of a ship); (in Prosody) a spondee; (in Geom.) the hypothenuse (of a triangle), diagonal (of a tetragon)
more: n. m. One who appraises the standing crops. Kanyan ke das hal chaleṅ, kanyan kā baṛ ser, muraliyā bole . Prov. The appraiser can command ten ploughs, his seer is over standard weight, all pipe to his tune. kànkūt', kanhāī , n. f. Valuation of the standing crop.
subs: or kānā-phūsī, s.f. Whispering into the ear, whispering, speaking in a low tone; consultation, advice; plot, intrigue; — kānā-bātī karnā or kānā-phūsī karnā (-kī), To whisper; to make a startling noise in the ear (of a child); to consult, to advise; to tell tales (of); to intrigue
(or uṛe-) jānā, v.n. To be deafened by noise, or by the constant dinning of a thing into the ears
s.f. Whispering into the ear; — kānā-kānī karnā (-kī), To whisper (to)
(-ke), To pull or twist the ears (of); to reprove; to chastise
s.m. The (Hindū) ceremony of shaving a child's head for the first time before boring his ears
(kisī-ke), To have a rmging in the ears; the ears to tingle or burn; to fancy that one hears a sound
(apne), To make (oneself) deaf, to shut (one's) ears; to turn a deaf ear (to, -par ?)
(-ke), To fill (one's) ears (with, -se), to excite dissension by tale-bearing, to poison the ear (of), to tell tales (of), to backbite
v.n. Pus or matter to run from the ear
(-ke), lit. 'A louse not to move on the ears'; to be utterly heedless (of), to be very negligent or careless; to produce no effect (on)
v.t. 'To place in the ear'; to remember; to abstain
(apne), To place the hands on the ears; to disclaim; to refuse, to deny
(-ke), To fall on the ear (of); to come to the ears (of); to be heard, to be audible
(-ko), To make one rise and sit alternately with his hands holding his ears; to have (one) completely under the thumb

(apnā), To hold (one's own) ears (by way of punishment); to lay hold of the ear (by way of contrition, &c.), to express contrition (for), to vow never to do a thing again (e.g. maiṅ kān pakaṛtā hūṅ, 'I lay hold of my ear,' the word being accompanied by the action); to confess inferiority
To shake or flap the ears (a dog, &c., — regarded as a bad omen for a journey)
v.n. To be or become deaf; to be deafened (by)
(-ke), To deafen (with a noise); to make a great or deafening noise
(-ke), To breathe or whisper into the ear (of); to tell tales, to poison the ear (of), to slander; to excite quarrels; to tutor
To disclose a secret, to blab; — to say stinging or bitter things
s.m. pl. Ear-rings studded with pearls or jewels worn over the ears (instead of hanging from the lobes of the same)
To incline the ear, to desire to hear
adj. Having ears, with ears, eared; auriculate, auriculated
(or dabāʼe) ćalā jānā, v.n. 'To make off with the ears down,' to slink or run away
To turn back the ears (as a horse does when preparing to bite)
(apnā), 'To show the ears'; to have the ears cleaned or the wax removed
(-par), or kān denā (-par ?), To give ear (to), to hear, listen (to), attend (to); to be attentive
(or dhar-ke) sunnā, or kān de-ke (or de) sunnā, v.t. To hear or listen to attentively
(-par), To lend an ear (to), to listen, attend (to), heed; — to put the ear (to), to listen, to play the eavesdropper
or kān-kā ḍholnā, s.m. The tympanum, drum of the ear
(-ke), To cut off the ears (of); — to get the better (of), to surpass, overcome, outwit
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Listening readily to what is said, easily deceived, credulous; — a soft or credulous person
(-ke), To worry or pain the ears (of); to din into the ears (of); to worry, pester, tease
v.n. To prick up the ears
(-ke), To open the ears (of); to bring to the knowledge (of), to inform, make acquainted (with), apprize; to prepare (one for), to caution, warn
v.n. To have a humming or buzzing noise in the ears; to become partially deaf
(-ke), To get at the ear (of), get into the confidence (of); — to be attentive; — to have a sore in the ear, have a bad ear
To flap or shake the ears
or kān malnā (-kā), To twist the ear (of); — to admonish, punish, chastise
s.m. Ear-wax; — kān-mail-wālā, or kān-mailiya, or kan-mailiyā, s.m. A person whose employment it is to pick or clean the ears
v.n. To stop (one's) ears, to turn a deaf ear (to)
(-ke), To happen to hear, to overhear, to catch the sound (of); to be heard, to be reported
v.n. 'To pour oil into the ear and sleep'; to be inattentive or negligent
To pour oil into the ears; to pretend not to hear
v.n. To have lumps of ear-wax in the ear; to be deaf (to)
(-ko) = kān dharnā, and kān-de sunnā, qq.v.
or kān na khuṛkhuṛānā, v.n. Not to move the ears; to keep the ears hanging motionless; to make no sign; to be silent; to be tamed, to be docile
(apne), To move the ears; to make a sign of acquiescence, to acquiesce
(-ke), 'To have ears'; to understand, comprehend; to get a lesson, to be warned by experience
(apne), To hear with one's own ears
(-ko), To know nothing at all of, to be totally ignorant of
(-ko), Not to hear at all or in the least
kānoṅ-meṅ uṅgliyāṅ denā = kān-meṅ uṅglī denā
kānoṅ-men bol mārnā = kān-meṅ bāt mārnā
is kān sunnā us kān uṛā-denā
(-ko), To take in at one ear and let out at the other, to pay no attention (to), to disregard.

def: s.m. Edge, rim; projection or handle (on the side of a vessel, &c., or one of two handles on the sides); one of the notches on the two sides of the upper leather of a shoe; — the part of a paper kite to which the string is tied
more: n. m. pl. kanne . 1. Rim; edge. 2. The two sides of the upper leather of a shoe. 3. The part of a paper kite to which the string is tied. 4. The rings attached to a metallic vessel. Kaṛhāī kā kannā. kannā nikal jānā , v. n. To be broken (the rim or edge). kannoṅ se ukhaṛnā , v. n. 1. To be cut (a paper kite). 2. To turn away with displeasure; to be cuts with. Tum to zarā sī bāt meṅ kannoṅ se ukhaṛ jāte ho .
s. m. 1. The notches on the two sides of the upper leather of a shoe. 2. The part of a paper kite to which the string is tied.
subs: vulg. kanne-dār, s.m. A shoe or slipper with a notch at the side, or with a projecting piece at the heel (to pull it on by)
(-kā), The rim or edge, or the handle (of a vessel), to be broken
(The string of a paper kite) to come clean out from, or be cut off at, the parts where it is tied; (met.) to fly off in a dudgeon, to turn away in a huff.

def: s. m. A kind of shoe or slipper.

ety: A. قنارة qinnārat; prob. an arabicized word
def: s.m. A slaughter-house; shambles; — a row of iron hooks on which butcher's meat is suspended; a butcher's shop.
more: s. m. Shambles where meat is exposed to sale.

ety: fr. kans, 'sweeping,' inf. n. of کنس
def: s.m. A sweeper; (met.) an avaricious man, a miser.
more: n. m. کنس he swept. 1. A sweeper. 2. A miser.
(adj. superl. of کنس Chald. To sweep) s. m. 1. A sweeper. 2. ( met. ) An avaricious ill-tempered person.

def: s.f. The little finger.
s.f. Outer edge, border, hem (see kannā); — a fillet, or a make-weight, attached to the lighter side of a paper-kite
more: n. f. 1. Border; hem. 2. A make-weight attached to the lighter end or car of a paper kite. 3. Broken leaves of tobacco. Tambākū kī kannī. kannī bāṅdhnā , v. a. To tie a fillet to the ear of a paper kite. kannī-dār , adj. Having a hem; embroidered. kannī dabnā , v. n. To submit; own one's power or superiority. Unkī mujh se kannī dabtī hai. kannī khānā . To fall to one side (a paper kite).

s. f. 1. A spark of a diamond or other gem. 2. Bran. کني کاٿنا kannī kāṭnā, To undermine a person, or effect his ruin by clandestine means. ¶ kin-e, pron. (dakh.) Any one, who? See کوٴي koʼī and کن kin.
subs: (-meṅ), To tie a fillet as a make-weight to the lighter side of a paper-kite
adj. Having a border or hem
(-kī), The power (of a person) to submit (to, -se, that of another)
To lean over, to incline to one side (a paper kite).

def: s.f. (dialec.), The ear of a horse.
s.f. The bow (of a paper kite); a slender waist; — tusk (of a boar).
more: n. f. 1. The bow of a paper kite. 2. ( Poet .) A thin waist ( patlī kamar ). 3. The tusks of a boar.
s. m. (dakh. i. q. نحوست) Bad luck, misfortune. کانپ مارنا kāmp mārnā, A misfortune to be or happen.

def: s.m. (dialec.) Cutting; a cut, slice, piece, bit (of fruit, &c.); an ear-ornament (of women); a deposit of mud (formed by a river or water-course); — a stroke of adverse fortune, misfortune, ill-luck
n. f. The temple. kanpaṭī kā hāḍ . ( Anat .) The temporal bones, or bones of the temples.
s.f. The temple; the upper part of the side of the face (between the temple and the ear)
more: s. f. The temple (the upper part of the side of the face).

def: s.m. = kāʼephal, q.v.
more: s. m. Name of a fruit. See کايپهل kāʼephal.

def: s.m. One whose ears are slit; — a kind of Hindū faqīr, with slit ears.
s.m. One whose ears are slit; — a kind of Hindū faqīr, with slit ears.
more: s. m. A kind of jogī or Hindū faḳīr with split ears.

def: s.m. Swelling of the glands between the ear and the throat.
E. n. m. Swelling of the glands between the ear and throat. kan-phaṭā, kan-phaṛā , n. m. A sect of Hindu faqirs with split ears.
s.m. One who blows or breathes into the ear (of another); any religious teacher (among the Hindūs) who whispers his instructions in the ears of his followers.

ety: Prk. कंपणअं; S. कम्पनीयं, rt. कम्प्
def: v.n. To tremble, shake, quake, quiver, shiver
more: v. a. S. कम्प, tremble. Pr. kampnam . To shake ( tharrānā ). Jijī! chhoran kā bābū to chho huā, aur manne kāṅp lag gaī . Rus. Wom. Sister, the child's father grew angry, and I began to tremble all over.
v. n. To shiver, to tremble, to quake, to shake.
subs: v.n. To break into trembling, to begin to tremble, or shake, &c.
ex: وہ اتنا خوفزدہ ہو چکا تھا کہ وہ کانپنے سے رک نہیں سکا۔
He became so scared that he could not stop trembling.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀁𑀧𑀇 (kaṃpaï), from Sanskrit कम्पते (kampate). Cognates include Gujarati કંપવું (kampvũ), Marwari कांपणौ (kā̃mpṇau), Assamese কঁপা (kõpa), Bengali কাঁপা (kãpa).

def: adj. & s.m. = kānā, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), One-eyed, monoculous, blind of one eye
subs: (as a cowrie, or fruit); having a black speck or spot outside (fruit); having a rotten kernel, or having no kernel (fruit); — foolish, stupid; — a one-eyed person; a perforated or specked fruit; the ace (at dice); — a stupid person

def: adj. काण, Pālī, kāno . 1. Blind of one eye; one-eyed. 1. Kāṅṛe, khore, kūbṛe, kuṭil, kuchālī jāni, Tiya bishekh, puni cheri, kahi Bharat mātu muskāni . Ramāyan. One-eyed, squint-eyed, crook-backed, black heart, ill ways, all these I've known, But woman's worse, slave girl worse still, Kekaī doth smiling own. 2. Kāṅṛe kī māṅ dāṅṛeṅ bhūne, kāṅṛāṅ pīṭe peṭ, Vohī kāreṅ kī leṭṛī, aur vohī kāṅṛeṅ kā khet . Rus. The one-eyed's mother parches grain, the son his stomach beats, Here too the one-eyed sleeps, and there the field of which he eats. 3. Ek tau kānī thī, dūsre paṛ gayā kunak . Prov. She had but one eye, and in it grit must fall. (Misfortunes never come single). 2. The rotten kernel of a nut, etc. Kaise kāne khudre ber uṭhā lāyā! kāṅṛā raggī , n. m. A wicked or headstrong one-eyed person. kāṅṛī maṭakko , n. f. A one-eyed wanton. kāṅṛī yā kānī kauṛī , n. f. 1. A kauṛi with a hole in it; a bad penny. 2. Good for nothing.
s.m. Taking pleasure in hearing (music, &c.); the pleasure afforded to the ear (by music, &c.).
more: s. m. Taking a pleasure in hearing (musick, &c.).

def: n. m. The pleasure of hearing music, etc.
s.m. One who takes pleasure in listening to music, a lover of music; an amateur in music.
more: s. m. A lover of musick, an amateur.

def: s.f. A rafter, beam; yoke (of a vehicle, or a plough); — ulceration on the soles of the feet of an elephant ( = kāṅḍī).
s.f. Name of a musical mode or rāginī, and of a measure in versification.
more: s. f. Name of a musical mode or rāginī.

ety: kāṅṛ˚ = Prk. कंड इ) or कंडे(इ) = S. कण्डय(ति), rt. कण्ड्
def: v.t. To tread on, trample; to crush.
more: v. a. To tread, to trample.

def: s.f. A tall species of grass that grows luxuriantly on waste and sandy soils, Imperata spontanea; — also = kās, q.v.
more: n. m. S. काश A tall grass, growing luxuriantly on neglected and sandy soils; Imperata spontanea , Wat. Phūleu kāṅs sakal mahi chhāī , Rāmāyan. Janu barkhā krat praghaṭ buṛhāī . The tall grass flowers so white which strew the plain, Like hoary locks of rains upon the wane. kāṅs meṅ tairnā , v. n. 1. To be deluded by a mirage. 2. To soar on the wings of fancy.
s. f. A kind of grass. See s. کاس.
subs: 'To swim in the long grass called kāṅs'; to move swimmingly along in fancy; to soar on the wings of fancy; to be deluded by a mirage.

def: s.m. A Rājā of Mathura (he was the cousin and implacable enemy of Krishṇa, by whom he was slain; as the foe of the deity, he is identified with the Asur Kālanemī).
more: n. prop. The uncle and enemy of Kṛish̤ṇa by whom he was slain. See under کشن kishan.

ety: Prk. कंसओ; S. कंस or कांस्य +कः
def: s.m. Bell-metal; any amalgam of zinc and copper; white copper; queen's metal; brass; — a drinking vessel (of bell-metal, or brass, &c.), a cup, goblet, bowl; a large flat dish
subs: s.m. A brasier, a maker of cups, goblets, &c.

ety: Prk. कंसओ; S. कंस or कांस्य +कः
def: s.m. Bell-metal; any amalgam of zinc and copper; white copper; queen's metal; brass; — a drinking vessel (of bell-metal, or brass, &c.), a cup, goblet, bowl; a large flat dish
more: (prop. کاسه) s. m. A cup, bowl, glass, goblet. کانسه گر kāṅsa-gar, One who makes cups, bowls, &c.
subs: s.m. A brasier, a maker of cups, goblets, &c.

def: s.f. A kind of scolopendra or julus (said to get into the brain through the ears, and by its bite to occasion excessive pain and even death).
s.f. = kan-salāʼī, q.v. s.f. A kind of scolopendra or julus (said to get into the brain through the ears, and by its bite to occasion excessive pain and even death).
n. f. A kind of scolopendra or julus which is said to get into the brain through the ears, and by its bite to occasion great pain and even death. kan suiyāṅ lenā , v. n. To eavesdrop.
s. f. A kind of Scolopendra or Julus which is said to get into the brain through the ears, and by its bite to occasion excessive pain and even death.
s.f. Bell-metal, &c. ( = kāṅsā).
more: n. f. S. कांस्य Bell metal. kāṅs'ah , n. m. 1. A large flat dish. 2. See khāsah. An dātā! kāṅsā taiyār hai. kāṅsah-kharch . Table expenses.

def: s.f. Hearing, listening, eavesdropping
s.m. A worm that destroys or injures young sugar-cane.
more: v. n. A worm that injures young īkh .

def: adj. Consisting of white copper, &c.; made of brass; — s.m. Bell-metal; brass, &c. ( = kāṅsā); a metallic vessel or cup
subs: s.m. A worker in bell-metal; a brasier, a pewterer
s.m. Blue vitriol (considered as a collyrium).

def: s.m. One beloved, a husband; a lover, a sweetheart
subs: s.m. The being a husband, the state or condition of husband; the disposition and sentiments appropriate in a woman towards her beloved.

def: s.f. A beloved or lovely woman; a wife, a sweetheart, mistress; — loveliness, beauty, &c. ( = kānti).
more: n. m. S. कण्टक, Pr. kaṅṭao . See /?/khār (1, 2). 1. A thorn; prick. Arai! ko ṭūk khā le nā! ke kāṅṭoṅ māṅ hāth paṛe saë ? Rus. Eat a little, friend! you have n't to plunge your hands in thorns. 2. A spur. 3. A fish-bone ( machhlī kā kāṅṭā ). 4. A porcupine's quill. Se kā kānṭā . 5. A fork. 6. A hook and eye. Mīyāṅ! botāmoṅ se to kāṅṭe lagā lo! 7. A fishing hook. 8. A pitchfork. 9. Small scales. 10. Dryness or roughness of the tongue; a furred tongue. Zabān par kāṅṭe paṛ gaë . 11. ( Met .) An eyesore; enemy. 12. ( Arith .) Proof; test. Kāṅṭā kar lo! sahīh galat mālūm ho jāëgā . 13. Adhesion; stickiness. Chāshnī kā kānṭā. kāṅṭā ḍālnā , v. a. To let hooks down a well, etc. to bring any thing up. kāṅṭā sā khaṭaknā , v. a. 1. To prick as a thorn. 2. To rankle in the breast. kāṅṭā sā nikal jānā , v. n. lit . to be taken out (a thorn). To be freed from distress or pain. kāṅṭā lagnā, yā chhubnā …

def: s.m. A thorn, prickle, spine; needle or pricker (of a gun, &c.); a nail (syn. kīl); sting (of a scorpion, &c.); spur (of a cock, &c.), rowel (of a spur); bone (of a fish); quill (of a porcupine); hand (of a watch or clock); a fork (e.g. ćhuṛī-kāṅṭā, 'knife and fork'); a prong; a pitchfork; a type; tongue (of a balance, or a buckle, &c.); bolt (of a lock); a balance having a tongue, small scales (esp. such as are used by goldsmiths); a hook (to hang a thing on; and as in 'hook and eye'); a fish-hook; a hooked instrument (for pulling buckets out of water, &c.); (fig.) a thorn in the side, a hateful person, a troublesome fellow, a pest, plague; erection of the hairs of the body (from joy, fear, &c.), horripilation; dryness, or roughness, or eruption (on the tongue, or the body, after fever, &c.); a disease in the throat of birds; (in Arith.) a cross drawn to prove a multiplication sum; proving, proof (of a sum); — a kind of fire-work (a large hollow species of thorn filled with powder); — viscosity, stickiness, adhesiveness (e.g. ćāshnī-kā kāṅṭā); congelation, or crystal (of candy, &c.)
more: s. m. 1. A thorn, spine. 2. A fork. 3. Small (goldsmithʼs) scales. 4. A spur. 5. A type. 6. The tongue of a balance. 7. Fishing hook. 8. A fish-bone. کانٿا سا نکل جانا kānṭā sā nikal jānā, To be freed from distress or injury. کانٿون پر گهسيٿنا kānṭoṅ par ghasīṭnā (lit.) To drag upon thorns; to extol or exalt one above his merits. (Used by a person who from humility disclaims the praises bestowed on him). کانٿي بوني kānṭe bone, (lit. to plant thorns) To prepare distress or misfortune for oneʼs self.
hāthī kānṭā, A large hollow species of thorn filled with powder and exploded as a firework ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
subs: (-meṅ), To let down a hook or a hooked instrument (into a well, &c. in order to fish up anything)
v.n. To prick as a thorn; to rankle (in)
v.n. 'To be taken out as a thorn'; to be freed from distress, or pain, or injury
(-kā), To prove (a sum) by means of a cross (kāṅṭā)
(-ko), To strike with its prickles or fins (a fish)
s.m. A clerk who notes and registers weighments
(-kā), To extricate (one's) thorn (from, -se); to afford relief (to one in pain)

def: s.m. A canon, rule, regulation, law, legislative act, statute, ordinance; — a species of dulcimer or harp

To make laws; to legislate. qānūn banāne-vāloṅ kī jamāat . A parliament; senate; congress; diet; legislative council. qānūn par tāmīl k . To administer the law. qānūn par chalnā , v. a. Criminal law. qānūn-i-qatai . A positive law. qānūn kā hukam rakhnā . To have the force of law. qānūn ke khilāf k. , v. a. To break a law.

(Gr. κανων ) s. m. 1. Rule, regulation, statute, canon, institute, system. 2. A dulcimer, or harp. قانونگو ḳānūn-go, s. m. An officer in each district, acquainted with its customs, the nature of the tenures of the land, &c. قانونگوٴي ḳānūn-go-ī, s. f. The office of a ḳānūn-go, q. v.

more: n. m. 1. Skull; cranium. Mār khauṅsṛon sāre kā kapāl phoṛ dūṅgā! kaisā bigāṛā hāṅḍe saë ? Rus. Ho, sister's pimp! I'll brain you with tattered shoes and smash your head! at whose misguidance are you wandering about? 2. Head; forehead. 3. Fate ( bhāg , 3). 1. Jāo Naipāl, sāth jāë kapāl . E. Prov. To Nepal who go, their fate goes with them too. 2. Jarat bilokeu jabahīṅ kapālā, Bidh ke likhe aṅk nij bhālā, Nar ke kar āpan badh bāṅchī, Haṅseū jānī Bidh girā asāṅchī . Rāmāyan. His skull in flames, upon his forehead Brahm's lines he descried, 'Die by the hand of man?' He laughed as though great Brahm had lied. 3. Ankar saṅdūr dekh āpan kapār phoṛe . Prov. On other's foreheads sendūr seen, her own head cracks with bootless spleen. kapāl-kiryā , n. f. A Hin. funeral ceremony. ety: old H. करि; Prk. करे or करिआ; S. कृत्वा, rt. कृ
def: part. (of karnā), Having done, &c.; — adv. & postpn. As, like, after the manner of; by the name or title of; by reason of, in consequence of, on account of; in respect of, as regards, as to; by means of, by, with, through (the conj. part. kar prefixed to certain verbs serves to form a class of intensives, or completives, &c.; see s.v. karnā)
subs: or kar-ke, or kar-kar-kar, or kar-kar-ke (by redupl. of kar; see ke), part., adv. & postpn. = kar (kar-ke or kar-kar is the more common form. Joined to substantives and certain adjectives it forms adverbs, e.g. kram-karke, 'respectively,' 'in order' ( = kram-se), 'gradually'; — dhruva-karke, 'firmly;' — mukhya-karke, 'principally.' Sometimes it is annexed to adverbs to emphasize them, e.g. avaśya-kar or kar-ke, 'necessarily, &c.' It is sometimes used as synonymous with se, e.g. ādhīntāʼī-karke = adhīntāʼī-se; — jal-karke snān, 'bathing with water'; — jis-karke ( = jis-se), 'by whom'; — us-karke ( = us-se), 'by him'; (see also kram-karke, above). Phrases in which kar-ke occurs are numerous, e.g. ek-ek-karke, 'one after another,' 'one at a time'; — būṅd-būṅd-karke, 'drop by drop,' 'a drop at a time'; — bahut-karke, 'in great measure,' 'at the most'; — viśesh-karke, 'particularly,' 'especially'; — satya-karke, 'in fact,' 'verily').

def: n. f. Dim. kaṛhaiyā . 1. A frying pan. 1. Tel na miṭhāi, chūlhe dharī kaḍhāī . Rus. Nor oil nor sweets, the frypan on the fire! (To give a feast he's fain, in all the house no grain). 2. Chaṛhī kaṛhāī tel na āyā, kab āëgā ? Prov. The oil may just keep away if it comes not when the frypan is on the fire. (You are come a day after the fair). 2. See halvā . 3. An offering of sweetmeats. kaṛhāī bāṅdhnā , v. a. To cast a spell over a frying pan. kaṛāhī chāṭnā , v. a. To lick up the pot. ☞ The women have a superstition that licking the pot brings the ill luck of a fall of rain on the wedding procession of the person who is given to this habit. Hence, whenever such a contretemps occurs the women abuse the bridegroom for having licked the pot. kaṛāhī chaṛhnā , v. n. 1. To be put on the fire (a frying pan). 2. To be made (preparations for a feast.) kaṛāhī k. , v. a. To offer sweetmeats, etc. to a goddess. kaṛāhī meṅ hāth ḍālnā , An ordeal. The accused is required to take out a piece of gold which has been dropped into a vessel of boiling oil. If he can do this without being scalded he is held innocent.

About last night what can I say? while with my love I lay, Turned on my side crack went my pearl, the knot just cut away. 2. To crunch; munch; gulp down. Is bahuaṛ ne dekh-kar kyoṅ na uṭṭhe āg, Chane dīye the pīsne, kuṛak gaī nir-bhāg . To see this daughter-in-law it puts me all on fire, Some pulse given her to grind she swallowed up entire. kuṛaknā , adj. Crackling; brittle. kuṛaknā kāgaz hai .

To be beaten (drums). kaṛaknā woh naubat kā bājoṅ ke sāth, Garajnā woh dhaunsoṅ kā ḍankoṅ ke sāth . Mīr Hasan. 3. To thunder; thunder with a loud clap. def: s.m. Cause, motive, purpose, reason; principle, ground, source, basis; occasion; account, sake, behalf; — action; agency; — a father; — adv. or postpn. For the reason, because (of, -ke), on account (of), for the sake (of), &c.
more: n. m. S. कारण from कृ to do. 1. Cause (باعث 1, 3). 2. Sake; behalf ( khātir , 5). 1. Bhūk kaṭāve sīs ne, bhūk de jān bikāë, Baldā kāran bhūk ke kāṅdhe jūā uṭhāë . Rus. For stomachs heads cut off, for stomachs lives are given, For the stomach's sake the yoke is borne and oxen driven. 2. Sāsaṛ kāran baid bulāyā, Sauk kahe terā dhagṛā āyā . Rus. For my mother-in-law the doctor I called in, My co-wife says 'your lover is within.'
s. m. Cause, motive, reason, occasion, account.
subs: s.m. The cause of all things; an epithet of the Great Creator.
ex: یِہ سَب آپ کے کارَن ہُوَا۔
All this happened because of you.

def: s.m. Making, doing; committing (a crime); causing, producing, effecting, accomplishing
more: n. m. 1. The ear ( kān ). 2. See karval . 3. (A. qāidā ) The base of a triangle. 4. ( Gram .) The instrumental case. 5. ( Pros .) A spondee. karan-phūl , n. m. A kind of earring.
s. m. Lace cut into small pieces and put on a cap.
subs: act, deed; business, occupation ( = karma); means of action, instrument; an expedient; instrumentality; (in Gram.) the instrumental case; — cause, motive, reason, &c. ( = kāraṇ, q.v.); an organ of sense; — an astronomical division of time (of which there are eleven, seven movable and four fixed, and two of which are equal to a lunar day)
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit किरण (kiraṇa). First attested in c. 1518 as Middle Hindi کرن (krn /⁠kiran⁠/).

def: s.m. = karān, and kanāra, qq.v. s.m. Cause, motive, purpose, reason; principle, ground, source, basis; occasion; account, sake, behalf; — action; agency; — a father; — adv. or postpn. For the reason, because (of, -ke), on account (of), for the sake (of), &c.
subs: s.m. The cause of all things; an epithet of the Great Creator.

def: v.t. To cause to be done, cause to be made; to actuate, effect, make; to perform (sacrifice, &c.), to assist
more: v. a. Caus. of کرنا q. v.
v. a. To make: see the word in the foregoing part.

def: s. (dakh.) A kind of trumpet or clarion: see P. کرنا or A. قرناي.
v.t. (Braj) = karnā, q.v. s.f. A musical horn; the large brass trumpet which sounds the bass; a trumpet, bugle, clarion, &c.
adj. & s.m. Working; laborious; — one who manages a business, a manager, an agent, or officer (syn. kār-pardāz).
more: part. act. (of کاريدن To work) 1. adj. Laborious. 2. Working. 3. s. m. A manager, an agent or officer.

def: n. f. A shawl made of raw silk.
s.m. A clove, Amyris heptaphylla (syn. lauṅg).
more: n. m. A clove. Amyris heptaphylla , Wat.

def: s.m. Skull; head; — a bone; a rib; a water-cup made from a cocoa-nut.
s.m. See s.v. karnā. s.f. A musical horn; the large brass trumpet which sounds the bass; a trumpet, bugle, clarion, &c.
s. m. Doer, maker.
s.f. A surd or irrational number, a surd root (in Arith.).
part. adj. To be done or made, practicable, feasible; lawful to be done; proper or suitable to be done.
s.m. The fever-nut Caesalpinia bonducella, or Guilandina bonducella (syn. kaṭ-karanj); Galedupa arborea; — a brown colour ( = karanjoʼī); smut (in barley, maize, bājrā, &c.).
more: s. Name of a tree, Galedupa indica: see also کرنجوا karanjawā.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Blear-eyed.
